body: Reproduced this at Kaneohe Bay field test. Lost two transect entries before we realized the queue was dropping them. This is a P1 — volunteers have no idea it's happening.
Adding a pre-check on storage before each queue write. If available space < 50MB, surface a warning banner and pause queuing. PR up by EOD.
PR #88 merged. Tested with storage throttled to 40MB on a Pixel 7 — warning banner fires correctly and queue pauses. Moving to Done.
Confirmed this at the Waimanalo boat launch — toggled airplane mode off after the dive, app just sat there. Had to open it and manually tap Sync. Not great for volunteers who don't know to do that.
Looks like the NetInfo event listener is getting torn down when the app goes to background. Re-registering on app foreground resume should fix it. Picking this up for sprint 5.
Got bit by this at Hanauma Bay — waded 30m to transect 4 and the GPS field auto-populated coordinates from transect 1 where I was standing 10 minutes earlier. The stale cache problem is real.

PR #147 up. On GPS field focus, we now fire a fresh getCurrentPosition call with a 5-second timeout. Falls back to cached only if fresh fix fails. Please review — want to make sure timeout doesn't block form interaction.

Reviewed PR #147. Logic looks solid. One nit: the 5-second timeout might feel long on older iPhones with poor GPS lock. Can we surface a small 'Getting location…' spinner so volunteers know it's working? Otherwise LGTM.
PR #112 merged. Client-side resize now downsamples to max 2048px on the long edge before upload. Tested on iPhone 15 Pro with 16MB RAW-converted JPEGs — all uploading cleanly. Moving to Done.
Nalani — do you have the full Hawaiian name alias list ready? I want to add it to the species index seed data before I write the fuzzy lookup logic so we can test against real terms.

Yes! I have a spreadsheet with 47 species mapped to Hawaiian common names, English common names, and scientific names. Will drop the CSV in the #species-db Slack channel today. Humuhumunukunukuapuaʻa is definitely in there.
The pin render issue is very noticeable around the Kaneohe Bay grid — there are 34 monitoring sites in that polygon alone. Clustering is essential before we do the volunteer beta.

Field test data from the March Mokulua Islands survey: average battery at end of 2-hour session was 28% remaining (started at 100%). The 2-second GPS poll is definitely the culprit — profiling shows it's 60% of CPU wakeups.
Good data — that matches what I saw on the Pixel 8 profiler. Adaptive poll rate should bring it under 20% drain per 2 hours. Will pick this up in sprint 6.
DLNR specifically asked for the export to include GPS precision (±meters) column too. Can we add that alongside lat/lon? Shouldn't be much extra work if we store it at survey time.
Good call. We store accuracy_meters in the location object already, just not surfacing it in exports. I'll add gps_accuracy_m to the column spec. Updating the ticket description.

We caught this during the Kaʻa'awa community dive day — two volunteers were at the same transect marker and both submitted. Only one record appeared in the coordinator dashboard. This needs to be fixed before any coordinated multi-diver surveys.

The conflict key needs to be composite: site_id + volunteer_id + survey_start_time. That gives us uniqueness per diver per session. Last-write-wins only makes sense within a single volunteer's session, not across different volunteers. Architecture proposal coming.
Volunteer feedback from the Waimanalo group: three people thought the app crashed during sync because nothing moved on screen. A simple 'Uploading 3 of 7 photos…' would make a huge difference.
Agreed. Progress bar is mid-implementation. Using the upload event stream from the multipart upload endpoint — gives us per-file and overall percentage. Should land this sprint.
Confirmed with the DLNR data sharing coordinator — water temperature is mandatory in the updated protocol starting Q3 2026. We need this in before the beta volunteer rollout.
PR #118 merged. Water temp field added after depth in the abiotic section, accepts values in °C with one decimal place. Backend schema migration applied. Moving to Done.
